sectionsUnavailable: Exact Genetic Risk of Hair Loss detail/sample body extraction is not available yet.
No calibrated hair-loss risk model is supplied with this fixture.
Symptoms, scalp exam, family history, ancestry or sex applicability metadata, treatment history, screening context, and clinical diagnosis are not supplied.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived AR androgen receptor context row is available. This supports educational androgenetic alopecia background only and is not a diagnosis or a quantified hair-loss risk result.
resultRows.sourceLabel: MedlinePlus Genetics: Androgenetic alopecia / AR gene
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: AR is one gene associated with androgenetic alopecia, but pattern hair loss involves multiple genetic and non-genetic factors.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A summary-level pattern hair-loss genetic context row is available, but no calibrated risk model is supplied. The report therefore describes context and limitations rather than assigning a risk score.
resultRows.sourceLabel: MedlinePlus Genetics: Androgenetic alopecia / NHGRI polygenic risk score overview
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Pattern hair loss can cluster in families, but inheritance is complex and a validated model is needed before estimating personal risk.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A calibrated hair-loss risk score, sex-specific model, ancestry applicability metadata, symptoms, and clinical context are unavailable in this fixture.
resultRows.sourceLabel: MedlinePlus hair loss / DTC test limitations
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Hair loss has many possible causes, so this package does not diagnose a condition, quantify risk, or recommend treatment from derived genetic context alone.

resultRows.item: Wellness & Longevity Hair Loss & Baldness sample row
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sibling Wellness & Longevity sample labels the sample hair-loss and baldness panel as Very High, displays a 72% lifetime-risk label, and shows actionability, lifestyle, medication, screening, misconception, and Hamilton-Norwood sections.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This is observed sample-report structure only. It is not a local hair-loss risk estimate, diagnosis, treatment recommendation, medication recommendation, supplement recommendation, hormone finding, transplant assessment, screening instruction, or actionability result.

unavailableSections: Exact Genetic Risk of Hair Loss detail/sample body extraction is not available yet.
Calibrated personal hair-loss risk score and model calibration metadata are unavailable.
Symptoms, scalp exam, family history, ancestry or sex applicability metadata, and treatment history are unavailable.
Diagnosis, hormone status, PCOS context, prostate context, cancer risk, medication response, supplement advice, screening guidance, actionability, and transplant guidance are not inferred.
plainEnglishLimits: Pattern hair loss is influenced by genetic, hormonal, age-related, and environmental factors.
Direct-to-consumer trait tests can provide estimates or context, not certainty about whether, when, or how much hair loss will occur.
Hair loss can have causes that are not captured by this genetic fixture, so medical symptoms or rapid/unusual hair loss require appropriate clinical evaluation.
This report must not be used to diagnose hair-loss conditions or guide medications, supplements, hormones, hair transplants, or other treatment decisions.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample report labels the sample risk of significant hair loss and baldness as Very High, with a displayed 72% lifetime-risk label and a preventive-measures actionability panel.
sampleRows.description: The page includes clinical-significance, risk, actionability, lifestyle, medication, screening, misconception, and Hamilton-Norwood scale sections, with several sections hidden in the sample.
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from the Hair Loss & Baldness pages of the public Sequencing.com Wellness & Longevity sample PDF; used here as sibling output-structure evidence for Genetic Risk of Hair Loss, not as a direct Hair Loss report sample or local risk, treatment, medication, supplement, hormone, transplant, or diagnosis advice.

summary-level pattern hair-loss genetic context available; no calibrated risk model supplied
calibrated hair-loss risk score, sex-specific model, ancestry applicability metadata, symptom evidence, scalp exam, family history, and treatment history unavailable in this fixture
Sibling sample visible row: genotype A linked in the sample variant appendix to androgenic alopecia. This is observed sample content only, not a local finding.

missingInputs: Exact Genetic Risk of Hair Loss detail/sample body extraction has not been extracted.

No calibrated hair-loss risk score, model coefficients, validation population, sex-specific model, ancestry applicability metadata, symptoms, scalp exam, family history, or treatment history is supplied.

Inputs for alopecia areata, thyroid disease, medication-related hair loss, stress-related shedding, PCOS, prostate conditions, cancer treatment, or other non-pattern hair-loss causes are not supplied.
uncertainty: Androgenetic hair loss involves multiple genetic and non-genetic factors, and many risk factors remain unknown.
The clinical meaning of derived genetic context depends on model calibration, ancestry and sex applicability, age, symptoms, family history, and other non-genetic factors.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data is not included.
The Wellness & Longevity sample row is sibling output-structure evidence only and is not a local user finding.

This report is educational and does not diagnose androgenetic alopecia, male pattern baldness, female pattern hair loss, alopecia areata, thyroid disease, PCOS, prostate conditions, cancer risk, or hormone status.
This report does not recommend starting, stopping, or changing minoxidil, finasteride, dutasteride, supplements, hormone therapy, hair transplant, or any treatment.
Exact Genetic Risk of Hair Loss detail/sample body extraction is still pending.
